title: NegotiationContract
description: ACP v0.1 — The terms of a proposed agent-to-agent exchange. Time-bound. Signed by both parties before execution begins. Permanently logged to both agents' identity histories.

negotiation_id.description: Globally unique negotiation identifier. Immutable once created.

state.description: Current negotiation state. ACP v0.1 uses exactly four states. Simplicity is a design requirement.

initiator_agent_id.description: ACP agent_id of the agent initiating the negotiation.

counterparty_agent_id.description: ACP agent_id of the agent receiving the negotiation.

capability_id.description: The specific capability being negotiated for. Must match a capability_id in the counterparty's AgentIdentity.

offer.description: The current offer on the table. Replaced on each COUNTER state.

offered_rate.description: Proposed rate for this execution in settlement units.

settlement_unit.description: Unit of account. Must match or be convertible to counterparty's declared settlement_unit.

payment_condition.description: The condition under which funds are released. This feeds directly into the SettlementIntent.

condition_type.description: How success will be verified at settlement time. Declared upfront. No ambiguity later.

description.description: Plain language description of the success condition.

check_path.description: JSONPath expression to the value being checked in the execution output.

score_field.description: Field in execution output containing the quality score.

minimum_score.description: Minimum score required for funds to release.

approver_agent_id.description: Agent or human identity responsible for verification.

approval_timeout_ms.description: Time allowed for approval before auto-resolution triggers.

timeout_resolution.description: What happens if approval times out.

threshold_executions.description: Number of executions above which discount applies.

discounted_rate.description: Rate applied once threshold is exceeded.

sla_declaration.description: What both parties declare success looks like. Agreed before execution. No ambiguity at settlement.

max_latency_ms.description: Maximum acceptable latency for this execution.

retry_allowed.description: Whether the executing agent may retry on failure before settlement resolves.

partial_completion_policy.description: What happens if execution completes partially.

dispute_resolution.description: Deterministic arbitration rules. Defined upfront. No support tickets. No humans in the loop unless declared.

arbitration_mode.description: How disputes are resolved if condition verification is ambiguous.

resolution_sequence.description: Ordered list of resolution steps tried in sequence.

negotiation_history.description: Full history of offers and counters. Append-only. Every state transition recorded.

proposed_by.description: agent_id of the party making this proposal.

note.description: Optional machine-readable context for this state change.

signature.description: Ed25519 signature by the proposing agent over this history entry.

expires_at.description: Hard expiry of this negotiation. Every offer is time-bound. No open-ended negotiations.

accepted_at.description: Timestamp of ACCEPT state. Present only when state is ACCEPT.

settlement_intent_id.description: ID of the SettlementIntent created on ACCEPT. Links negotiation to settlement.

final_signature_initiator.description: Ed25519 signature by initiator over the final accepted contract. Required before SettlementIntent is created.

final_signature_counterparty.description: Ed25519 signature by counterparty over the final accepted contract. Required before SettlementIntent is created.

payment_condition.description: Release payment when confidence_score in output is >= 0.90
